what_it_does: Acceptance test that verifies node provenance layers are stored as data, not just visual styling: authored node-types default to ORIGIN='authored' and system/brain-written types carry ORIGIN='system'; Suite.state() surfaces a per-node layer field; and the brain's _tag_system_origin helper injects and deduplicates an ORIGIN='system' marker in generated module source.
observations: Defines a custom check(label, cond) helper that asserts and counts PASS globally.
Uses a temporary FsStore, discovers nodes from the repo nodes/ directory, and creates a Suite.
Creates a constant node and a wordcount node, then verifies by node id that state(g)['nodes'] carry layer='authored' and layer='system'.
Imports the private runtime.suite._tag_system_origin to test that it injects ORIGIN = 'system' and that repeated application is idempotent.
Docstring frames the feature as 'I4, context-13 "Layers"' and emphasizes layers are a DATA property.
standards: Treats provenance origin as a first-class data attribute on node types and node state.
Tests both the registry-level ORIGIN and the runtime state-level layer field.
Tests code-generation provenance by inspecting injected source text.
conventions: Uses a hand-rolled check() helper instead of pytest assertions.
Cleans up a temporary directory in a try/finally block.
Imports implementation-private helper _tag_system_origin for direct contract testing.
concerns: Relies on the private function _tag_system_origin, making the test sensitive to internal implementation changes.
Global mutable PASS counter is not thread-safe and mixes test state with module globals.
Hard-codes node type names ('constant', 'wordcount') and node ids without explaining where they come from.
notes: An acceptance test that locks in the contract that provenance layers are data attributes and that brain-generated modules carry an injected ORIGIN marker.
questions: How does the registry populate the ORIGIN attribute for each node type?
What exactly does Suite.state() use to derive the per-node layer field?
What are 'I4' and 'context-13 "Layers"' referring to in the project taxonomy?
Are all brain-written node types guaranteed to be tagged system, or only those created through propose_node?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is to prove layers are a data property surfaced per node; the file does exactly that by testing registry ORIGIN, state layer, and source-code injection.
intention_signals: Encode the rule that every node carries a provenance layer derived from who authored its type, so the canvas and downstream tools can filter or audit human-written versus system-generated parts of the graph.
novelty: It treats code-generation provenance as a node-level data layer and tests the brain's source-code injection mechanism directly for idempotency.
contribution: A concrete, runnable acceptance test that documents and enforces the provenance-layer contract: authored nodes default to 'authored', system-generated nodes are 'system', and the brain injects an idempotent ORIGIN marker into generated modules.
summary_for_embedding: Acceptance test verifying that node provenance layers (authored vs system) are stored as data attributes on node types and surfaced per node in suite state, and that the brain injects an ORIGIN='system' marker into generated modules.
intention_for_embedding: To lock in the contract that provenance origin is a data property, not visual styling, so the canvas and future consumers can toggle or audit layers.

symbols.does: Custom assertion helper that asserts a condition under a label, increments a global pass counter, and prints an ok message.
symbols.description: The check function is used throughout the acceptance test in place of a test framework assertion. It takes a human-readable label and a boolean condition, asserts the condition with a FAIL label on failure, increments the module-level PASS counter on success, and prints a formatted ok line to stdout.
